In the 2023 rugby league season, the [[York Knights]] competed in the [[2023 RFL Championship]], the [[2023 Challenge Cup]] and the [[2023 RFL 1895 Cup]]. This was the first season in which the team competed as the York Knights following the club changing its name to York RLFC from the York City Knights in October 2022.

York had an inconsistent first part of the season where after a strong start they were fourth in the league having won three of their opening five league games. They then had a run of four league defeats and put together back-to-back league wins once more before having a mid-season run of five losses that left York near the bottom of the table. Their league form changed in July from which point they won 10 of their final 11 league matches to finish in sixth-place and equal their position from the previous season. In the Challenge Cup, York reached the quarter-finals for the first time since 2004 and the team also qualified for the semi-finals of the 1895 Cup.

==Summary==
===Pre-season===
On 14 October 2022, the York City Knights announced the club had been renamed as York RLFC, with the men's team being known as the York Knights and the women's team being rebranded as [[York Valkyrie]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/york-city-knights-announce-brilliant-new-rebrand/ |title=York City Knights announce brilliant new rebrand |website=Total Rugby League |date=14 October 2022}}</ref> On 17 October, [[Andrew Henderson (rugby league)| Andrew Henderson]] was appointed as head coach replacing [[James Ford (rugby league)|James Ford]] who had recently left to take a coaching role at [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/andrew-henderson-appointed-york-coach-on-long-term-deal |title=Andrew Henderson appointed York coach on long-term deal |website=Love Rugby League |date=17 October 2022 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> In November, [[Danny Kirmond]] took on the role of player/assistant coach<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-kirmond-to-play-on-in-2023-alongside-new-coaching-role |title=Danny Kirmond to play on in 2023 alongside new coaching role |website=Love Rugby League |date=11 November 2022 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> and in January [[Ged Corcoran]] was also appointed as an assistant coach.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ged-corcoran-york-knights-ireland |title=Ireland coach Ged Corcoran is back in clubland with York |website=Love Rugby League |date=11 January 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref>
[[File:First York -York RLFC.jpg|thumb|upright=0.75|left|[[First York]] bus promoting York RLFC's re-branding<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkrlfc.com/post/first-bus-becomes-york-rlfc-partner-for-third-season |title=First Bus becomes York RLFC Partner for third season |website=York RLFC |date=4 May 2023 |access-date=19 October 2023}}</ref>|alt=a black double-decker bus decorated with yellow text and York RLFC logos]]
In a pre-season interview Henderson noted several factors that had influenced preparations for the upcoming campaign, including that the majority of new signings for the season had been made before his arrival, and that some of the players required time off to recover following the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup|2021 World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/andrew-henderson-coaching-return-york-knights |title=Andrew Henderson on return to coaching, York's recruitment and pre-season challenges |website=Love Rugby League |date=2 February 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> Several York players had been called up to represent [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup squads|national teams at the tournament]], which took place in October and November 2022, but before the start of the 2023 season two of them, [[Jacob Ogden]] and [[Brendan O'Hagan]], had asked to be released from their contracts at York.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/york-knights-2023-squad-numbers-ata-hingano-to-wear-no-6-jersey |title=York Knights 2023 squad numbers: Ata Hingano to wear No. 6 jersey |website=Love Rugby League |date=12 January 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref><ref name=LRL18jan23>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/brendan-ohagan-york-australia-ireland |title=Ireland star Brendan O'Hagan leaves York for Australia return |website=Love Rugby League |date=18 January 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref><ref name=YRLFC24nov22>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkrlfc.com/post/ogden-departs-the-knights |title=Ogden departs the Knights |website=York RLFC |date=24 November 2022 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref>

York played only one pre-season match, a 28–12 away win at League 1 side [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/list-of-2023-rugby-league-pre-season-friendlies |title=List of 2023 rugby league pre-season friendlies |website=Love Rugby League |date=6 February 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> The following week, their home friendly against [[Batley Bulldogs]] was cancelled due to the pitch being frozen.<ref name=LRL21jan23>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/weather-disrupts-pre-season-schedule-as-games-called-off |title=Weather disrupts pre-season schedule as games called off |website=Love Rugby League |date=21 January 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> At the start of February, ''Love Rugby League'''s season preview predicted York would repeat their 2022 season to finish sixth in the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/everything-you-need-to-know-2023-championship-season |title=Big Preview: Everything you need to know about the 2023 Championship season |website=Love Rugby League |date=1 February 2023}}</ref>
{{clear|left}}
===2023 season===
York's season began on 5 February with a home loss to [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. They then recorded a home win against [[Bradford Bulls]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/five-takeaways-from-yorks-win-over-bradford-in-the-championship |title=Five takeaways from York's win over Bradford in the Championship |website=Love Rugby League |date=13 February 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> before travelling to [[Toulouse Olympique]] where they lost 36–0.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23331477.york-knights-suffer-36-0-defeat-championship-leaders/ |title=York Knights suffer 36–0 defeat at Championship leaders |website=[[The Press (York)|York Press]] |date=18 February 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> Two league wins, against [[Barrow Raiders]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61453/betfred-championship-round-up-%7C-round-four |title=Betfred Championship Round-Up {{!}} Round Four |website=RFL |date=26 February 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> and [[Swinton Lions]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/23365351.henderson-really-pleased-knights-cruise-past-swinton/ |title=Henderson 'really pleased' as Knights cruise past Swinton |website=York Press |date=6 March 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> were followed on 12 March by a win over NCL Division One side [[West Bowling A.R.L.F.C.|West Bowling]] in the third round of the [[2023 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]]. The match saw [[Matty Marsh]] make his 100th appearance for York.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkrlfc.com/post/york-knights-52-12-west-bowling-match-report |title=York Knights 52-12 West Bowling - Match Report |website=York RLFC |date=12 March 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref>
In mid-March, [[BBC Sport]] selected York's fourth round Challenge Cup match against [[Sheffield Eagles]] to be shown on its [[BBC iPlayer|iPlayer]] service. Both clubs were in the top four of the Championship at the time,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61530/bbc-sport-to-show-york-knights-v-sheffield-eagles-clash |title=BBC Sport to show York Knights v Sheffield Eagles clash |website=RFL |date=17 March 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> but York then lost their next two league games slipping to ninth in the table with head coach Andrew Henderson commenting that York's "performances certainly haven't been at the standard that we expect from ourselves as a group".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61571/york-boss-henderson-relishing-challenge-cup-distraction |title=York boss Henderson relishing Challenge Cup distraction |website=RFL |date=30 March 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61596/betfred-championship-preview-%7C-round-eight |title=Betfred Championship Preview {{!}} Round Eight |website=RFL |date=6 April 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> In the cup match, York had a 20–0 lead over Sheffield when [[Danny Kirmond]], who had scored two of York's tries, was sent-off after 30 minutes, but the team held on to win the match 24–22.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23429644.12-man-knights-see-off-sheffield-eagles-24-22-challenge-cup/ |title=12-man Knights see off Sheffield Eagles 24-22 in Challenge Cup |website=York Press |date=2 April 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref>

Back in the Championship, York's run of losses continued with a 22–10 defeat to [[Keighley Cougars]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23443355.knights-second-best-classy-keighley-22-10-defeat/ |title=York second best against classy Keighley in 22-10 defeat |website=York Press|date=7 April 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> and an away loss to [[Halifax Panthers]]. that saw York drop to tenth in the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23459005.injury-hit-knights-defend-resolutely-16-6-defeat-halifax/ |title=Injury-hit Knights defend resolutely in 16-6 defeat at Halifax |website=RFL |date=16 April 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> York then had a series of home matches against two teams that they had done well against in recent meetings; [[Newcastle Thunder]] and [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61705/betfred-championship-preview-%7C-round-10 |title=Betfred Championship Preview {{!}} Round 10 |website=RFL |date=4 May 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61721/betfred-championship-preview-%7C-round-11 |title=Betfred Championship Preview {{!}} Round 11 |website=RFL |date=11 May 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> On 21 April, York defeated Newcastle 22–18 in the fifth round of the Challenge Cup. The win also earned York a place in the semi-finals of the [[2023 RFL 1895 Cup|1895 Cup]] later in the year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23473433.knights-one-win-wembley-thrilling-22-18-win-newcastle/ |title=Knights one win from Wembley after thrilling 22-18 win over Newcastle |website=York Press |date=21 April 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> York then won 30–28 against London in a match played as a double header with the [[2023 Women's Challenge Cup|Women's Challenge Cup]] match between [[York Valkyrie]] and {{rlw team|hull kr}}. For the matches, which took place on Heritage Weekend, both York teams wore shirts in black and gold hoops inspired by the [[York Wasps|York]] side that reached the semi-finals of the [[1983–84 Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkrlfc.com/post/heritage-jersey-revealed-ahead-of-2023-heritage-weekend |title=Heritage Jersey revealed ahead of 2023 Heritage Weekend |website=York RLFC |date=6 May 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref>
The Knights followed this with a league win over Newcastle<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23521462.knights-just-enough-26-22-win-newcastle-thunder/ |title=York do just enough in 26-22 win over Newcastle Thunder |website=York Press |date=14 March 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> before recording a 36–12 win over London in the sixth round of the cup which saw York qualify for the quarter-finals for the first time since [[2004 Challenge Cup|2004]] and become the only remaining Championship side in the competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65615512 |title=Challenge Cup: York Knights 36-12 London Broncos - Olly Butterworth scores debut hat-trick |work=BBC Sport |date=21 May 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref>

York were back at the [[York Community Stadium]] the next week, this time as the nominal away side as they faced [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[Summer Bash]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/summer-bash-round-up-london-toulouse-bradford-derby |title=Summer Bash round-up: London beat Toulouse as Bradford win derby |website=Love Rugby League |date=28 May 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> Their 46–16 defeat to the team at the top of the league was the start of a run of five losses in the Championship,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61886/betfred-championship-preview-%7C-round-17 |title=Betfred Championship Preview {{!}} Round 17 |website=RFL |date=29 June 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> during which time York were also knocked out the Challenge Cup at the quarter-finals stage. The cup match was played alongside the women's match between York Valkyrie and [[Cardiff Demons]], on what was the first time that a double-header of men's and women's Challenge Cup quarter-final matches had been hosted at one venue.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/23595194.york-knights-york-valkyries-cup-quarter-finals-make-history/ |title=York Knights and York Valkyrie's Cup Quarter Finals make history |website=York Press |date=16 June 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> York Knights held the lead against Super League side [[Leigh Leopards]] until the last twenty minutes but went on to lose 34–14 and exited the competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61869/betfred-championship-preview-%7C-round-16 |title=Betfred Championship Preview {{!}} Round 16 |website=RFL |date=22 June 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref>

York's from began to improve at the start of July with a 28–12 win over [[Halifax Panthers]] which moved them up to tenth in the league and seven points off the bottom of the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61893/betfred-championship-round-up-%7C-round-17 |title=Betfred Championship Round-Up {{!}} Round 17 |website=RFL |date=3 July 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> This was followed by wins against Keighley Cougars<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/23644668.keighley-cougars-trouble-york-rlfc-humble/ |title=Keighley Cougars in trouble after York RLFC humble them |website=Telegraph & Argus |date=10 July 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> and Sheffield Eagles that moved York up to ninth place.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23658999.knights-earn-third-straight-win-23-18-victory-sheffield-eagles/ |title=Knights earn third straight win in 23-18 victory over Sheffield Eagles |website=York Press |date=16 July 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> On 23 July, York played in the semi-final of the 1895 Cup against Batley who were third in the Championship at the time. York could not recover from a 14–0 deficit at half-time and went on to lose the match 22–8.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dewsburyreporter.co.uk/news/people/batley-bulldogs-seal-first-ever-wembley-appearance-with-brilliant-1895-cup-semi-final-win-over-york-knights-4228857 |title=Batley Bulldogs seal first ever Wembley appearance with brilliant 1895 Cup semi-final win over York Knights |website=Dewsbury Reporter |date=23 July 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61955/preview-%7C-ab-sundecks-1895-cup-semi-finals |title=Preview {{!}} AB Sundecks 1895 Cup Semi Finals |website=RFL |date=21 July 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> A win over London Broncos then gave York a run of four successive league wins before they hosted second-placed Toulouse at the start of August. After trailing 12–4 at the interval, York fought back to take a two-point lead before Toulouse scored with 10 minutes remaining to win 18–14.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23704051.knights-winning-streak-ends-14-18-defeat-toulouse-olympique/ |title=Knights' winning streak ends with 14-18 defeat to Toulouse Olympique |website=York Press |date=5 August 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> York's fightback in their match against Widnes proved more successful when they came from 20–12 down at half-time to win 40–30.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23735418.york-knights-stun-widnes-vikings-40-30-thrilling-fightback/ |title=York Knights stun Widnes Vikings 40-30 in thrilling fightback |website=York Press |date=20 August 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> After the match, York were still in ninth place, but were now on 20 points and with five matches remaining were still in contention for the play-offs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62073/the-race-for-the-betfred-championship-play-offs |title=The Race for the Betfred Championship Play-Offs |website=RFL |date=24 August 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref>

York followed this with a 20–6 win at Newcastle Thunder<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23748894.york-knights-beat-newcastle-thunder-20-6-winning-run-continues/ |title=York Knights beat Newcastle Thunder 20-6 as winning run continues |website=York Press |date=26 August 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> and started September with a 26–22 home win over Swinton Lions.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23764752.york-knights-tested-scrappy-26-22-victory-swinton-lions/ |title=York Knights tested in scrappy 26-22 victory over Swinton Lions |website=York Press |date=3 September 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> A 20–10 victory at Bradford Bulls<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23780018.york-knights-keep-play-off-bid-alive-20-10-win-bradford-bulls/ |title=York Knights keep play-off bid alive with 20-10 win at Bradford Bulls |website=York Press |date=10 September 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> kept the winning run going for York as they headed into their round 26 match against Batley. After going 10–0 down, York recovered to go in 14–10 at the interval. Batley then levelled midway through the second half but a late drop goal from [[Liam Harris]] was enough to give York a 15–14 win and moved them up to seventh in the table just a point behind Halifax.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/matchreports/23797902.harris-steers-york-knights-dramatic-15-14-win-batley-bulldogs/ |title=Harris steers York Knights to dramatic 15-14 win over Batley Bulldogs |website=York Press |date=18 September 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62156/betfred-championship-preview-%7C-round-27 |title=Betfred Championship Preview {{!}} Round 27 |website=RFL |date=21 September 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> York then won their final match of the regular season away to Barrow and, with a Halifax loss to Swinton, they moved up into sixth-place to claim the final play-off spot.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62179/betfred-championship-round-up-%7C-round-27 |title=Betfred Championship Round-Up {{!}} Round 27 |website=RFL |date=25 September 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> In the opening round of the play-offs, York travelled to Bradford where after taking an 8–0 lead, York could not withstand a Bradford fightback that ended York's season with a 22–8 defeat.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62199/betfred-championship-round-up-%7C-1-october |title=Betfred Championship Round-Up {{!}} 1 October |website=RFL |date=2 October 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref>

===Post-season===
At the end-of-year awards, Andrew Henderson was nominated as Championship Coach of the Year and [[AJ Towse]] was nominated for Championship Young Player of the Year for a second time.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/championship-award-nominations-announced-with-six-clubs-represented-across-three-categories |title=Championship award nominations announced with six clubs represented across three categories |website=Love Rugby League |date=28 September 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> Both lost out to London Broncos' nominees [[Mike Eccles]] (coach) and [[Bill Leyland]] (young player), however there was some success for York RLFC with York Valkyrie, who had won the [[2023 RFL Women's Super League|Women's Super League]], picking up the Coach of the Year (Lindsay Anfield) and [[Woman of Steel]] awards ([[Sinead Peach]]),<ref>{{cite web |title=Bevan French: Wigan Warriors stand-off wins 2023 Steve Prescott MBE Man of Steel award |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67070647 |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=22 May 2026 |date=10 October 2023}}</ref> and York's wheelchair team winning the Wheelchair Rugby League Club of the Year Award.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkrlfc.com/post/york-rlfc-win-wheelchair-club-of-the-year-award |title=York RLFC win Wheelchair Club of the Year Award |website=York RLFC |date=16 October 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref>

In October, York were awarded a provisional Grade B ranking under the newly introduced [[IMG Grading for the British Rugby Football League|IMG Grading]] system. Their score of 10.05 placed them at 17th in the rankings.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/23878016.york-knights-short-super-league-spot-imgs-provisional-grading/ |title=York Knights short of Super League spot in IMG's provisional grading |website=York Press |date=25 October 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref>

York captain [[Chris Clarkson]], who had joined the club ahead of the 2020 campaign, announced that he would retire at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkrlfc.com/post/captain-clarkson-to-retire-at-the-end-of-2023 |title=Captain Clarkson to retire at the end of 2023 |website=York RLFC |date=16 September 2023 |access-date=26 May 2026}}</ref> Also announcing his retirement was [[Danny Kirmond]] who re-joined former club Wakefield in a coaching role.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wakefield-trinity-coach-daryl-powell-welcomes-danny-kirmond-back-to-belle-vue |title=Wakefield Trinity coach Daryl Powell welcomes 'club legend' back to Belle Vue in coaching role |website=Love Rugby League |date=8 November 2023 |access-date=22 May 2026}}</ref> Other departures included [[Pauli Pauli]], who had missed much of the season through injury,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/23920550.pauli-pauli-departs-york-knights-pursue-opportunities-elsewhere/ |title=Pauli Pauli departs York Knights to pursue opportunities elsewhere |website=York Press |date=13 November 2023 |access-date=25 May 2026}}</ref> and Matty Marsh who signed for Sheffield Eagles at the end of the year along with [[James Glover (rugby league)|James Glover]] who had been on loan there since July and made his move permanent for the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sheffieldeagles.com/Eagles-News/article/eagles-sign-knights-fullback-marsh/ |title=Eagles sign Knights full-back Marsh |website=Sheffield Eagles |date=6 November 2023 |access-date=26 May 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sheffieldeagles.com/Eagles-News/article/loan-eagle-glover-becomes-permanent/ |title=Loan Eagle Glover becomes permanent |website=Sheffield Eagles |date=8 November 2023 |access-date=26 May 2026}}</ref>

===Challenge Cup / 1895 Cup===
{{main|2023 Challenge Cup|2023 RFL 1895 Cup}}
